questionHtml: When there is no vaccination, the estimated number of cases for lower-middle income countries is larger than in either low income or upper-middle income countries. Suggest one reason for this.
markschemeHtml:
1. higher population in lower-middle income countries/subgroup (compared to the other 2 subgroups);
  2. low income population may not have/have less access to medicine/antibiotics/vaccination (compared to lower-middle income population)/ low-income populations may not report data
    OR
    upper-middle population may have better living conditions/more adequate medical diagnoses (compared to lower-middle income population);

Lower-middle income subgroup is not the same as LMIC (includes all 3 subgroups). LMIC should not be used as equivalent.

Mpb: accept vice versa.
